options.name: --password
options.description:
Authentication string for password
authentication.

options.name: --cluster-username
options.description:
Username to authenticate
intra-cluster communication with.

options.name: --cluster-password
options.description: Password to authenticate

options.name: --acl-file
options.description: External ACL user file.

options.name: --aad-authority
options.description: (Default: "https://login.microsoftonline.com") The authority of AAD authentication.

options.name: --aad-audiences
options.description:
The audiences of AAD token for AAD
authentication. Should be a comma
separated string.

options.name: --aad-issuers
options.description: The issuers of AAD token for AAD

options.name: --aad-authorized-app-ids
options.description: The authorized client app Ids for AAD

options.name: --aad-validate-acl-username
options.description:
(Default: False) Only valid for AclWithAAD mode.
Validates username -  expected to be
OID of client app or a valid group's
object id of which the client is part
of.

options.name: --aof
options.description:
(Default: False) Enable write ahead logging
(append-only file).

options.name: --aof-memory
options.description:
(Default: "64m") Total AOF memory buffer used in bytes
(rounds down to power of 2) - spills
to disk after this limit

options.name: --aof-page-size
options.description:
(Default: "4m") Size of each AOF page in bytes(rounds
down to power of 2)

options.name: --aof-commit-freq
options.description:
(Default: 0) Write ahead logging (append-only
file) commit issue frequency in
milliseconds. 0 = issue an immediate
commit per operation, -1 = manually
issue commits using COMMITAOF command

options.name: --aof-commit-wait
options.description:
(Default: False) Wait for AOF to flush the commit
before returning results to client.
Warning: will greatly increase
operation latency.

options.name: --aof-size-limit
options.description:
Maximum size of AOF (rounds down to
power of 2) after which unsafe
truncation will be applied. Left
empty AOF will grow without bound
unless a checkpoint is taken

options.name: --aof-refresh-freq
options.description:
(Default: 10) AOF replication (safe tail address)
refresh frequency in milliseconds. 0
= auto refresh after every enqueue.

options.name: --subscriber-refresh-freq
options.description:
(Default: 0) Subscriber (safe tail address)
refresh frequency in milliseconds
(for pub-sub). 0 = auto refresh after
every enqueue.

options.name: --compaction-freq
options.description:
(Default: 0) Background hybrid log compaction
frequency in seconds. 0 = disabled
(compaction performed before
checkpointing instead)

options.name: --compaction-type
options.description:
(Default: None) Hybrid log compaction type. Value
None - no compaction, Shift
- shift begin address without
compaction (data loss), Scan - scan
old pages and move live records to
tail (no data loss), Lookup - lookup
each record in compaction range, for
record liveness checking using hash
chain (no data loss)

options.name: --compaction-force-delete
options.description:
(Default: False) Forcefully delete the inactive
segments immediately after the
compaction strategy (type) is
applied. If false, take a checkpoint
to actually delete the older data
files from disk.

options.name: --compaction-max-segments
options.description:
(Default: 32) Number of log segments created on
disk before compaction triggers.

options.name: --obj-compaction-max-segments
options.description:
(Default: 32) Number of object store log segments
created on disk before compaction
triggers.

options.name: --lua
options.description: (Default: False) Enable Lua scripts on server.

options.name: --lua-transaction-mode
options.description:
(Default: False) Run Lua scripts as a transaction
(lock keys - run script - unlock
keys).

options.name: --gossip-sp
options.description:
(Default: 100) Percent of cluster nodes to gossip
with at each gossip iteration.

options.name: --gossip-delay
options.description:
(Default: 5) Cluster mode gossip protocol per node
sleep (in seconds) delay to send
updated config.

options.name: --cluster-timeout
options.description:
(Default: 60) Cluster node timeout is the amount of
seconds a node must be unreachable.

options.name: --cluster-tls-client-target-host
options.description:
(Default: "GarnetTest") Name for the client target host when
using TLS connections in cluster
mode.

options.name: --server-certificate-required
options.description:
(Default: True) Whether server TLS certificate is
required by clients established on
the server side, e.g., for cluster
gossip and replication.

options.name: --tls
options.description: (Default: False) Enable TLS.

options.name: --cert-file-name
options.description:
TLS certificate file name (example:
testcert.pfx).

options.name: --cert-password
options.description:
TLS certificate password (example:
placeholder).

options.name: --cert-subject-name
options.description: TLS certificate subject name.

options.name: --cert-refresh-freq
options.description:
(Default: 0) TLS certificate refresh frequency in
seconds (0 to disable).

options.name: --client-certificate-required
options.description:
(Default: True) Whether client TLS certificate is
required by the server.

options.name: --certificate-revocation-check-mode
options.description:
(Default: NoCheck) Certificate revocation check mode for
certificate validation (NoCheck,
Online, Offline).

options.name: --issuer-certificate-path
options.description:
Full path of file with issuer
certificate for validation. If empty
or null, validation against issuer
will not be performed.

options.name: --latency-monitor
options.description: (Default: False) Track latency of various events.

options.name: --metrics-sampling-freq
options.description:
(Default: 0) Metrics sampling frequency in
seconds. Value of 0 disables metrics
monitor task.

options.name: -q
options.description:
Enabling quiet mode does not print
server version and text art.

options.name: --logger-level
options.description:
(Default: Warning) Logging level. Value options: Trace,
Debug, Information, Warning, Error,
Critical, None

options.name: --logger-freq
options.description:
(Default: 10) (Default: 5) Frequency (in seconds)
of logging (used for tracking
progress of long running operations
e.g. migration)

options.name: --disable-console-logger
options.description: (Default: False) Disable console logger.

options.name: --file-logger
options.description:
Enable file logger and write to the
specified path.

options.name: --minthreads
options.description:
(Default: 0) Minimum worker threads in thread
pool, 0 uses the system default.

options.name: --maxthreads
options.description: (Default: 0) Maximum worker threads in thread

options.name: --miniothreads
options.description:
(Default: 0) Minimum IO completion threads in
thread pool, 0 uses the system
default.

options.name: --maxiothreads
options.description: (Default: 0) Maximum IO completion threads in

options.name: --network-connection-limit
options.description:
(Default: -1) (Default: -1) Maximum number of
simultaneously active network
connections.

options.name: --use-azure-storage
options.description:
(Default: False) Use Azure Page Blobs for storage
instead of local storage.

options.name: --storage-string
options.description:
The connection string to use when
establishing connection to Azure
Blobs Storage.

options.name: --checkpoint-throttle-delay
options.description:
(Default: 0) Whether and by how much should we
throttle the disk IO for checkpoints:
-1 - disable throttling; >= 0 - run
checkpoint flush in separate task,
sleep for specified time after each
WriteAsync

options.name: --fast-commit
options.description: (Default: True) Use FastCommit when writing AOF.

options.name: --fast-commit-throttle
options.description:
(Default: 1000) Throttle FastCommit to write metadata
once every K commits.

options.name: --network-send-throttle
options.description:
(Default: 8) Throttle the maximum outstanding
network sends per session.

options.name: --sg-get
options.description:
(Default: False) Whether we use scatter gather IO for
MGET or a batch of contiguous GET
operations - useful to saturate disk
random read IO.

options.name: --replica-sync-delay
options.description:
(Default: 5) Whether and by how much
(milliseconds) should we throttle the
replica sync: 0 - disable throttling

options.name: --replica-offset-max-lag
options.description:
(Default: -1) Throttle ClusterAppendLog when
replica.AOFTailAddress -
ReplicationOffset >
ReplicationOffsetMaxLag. 0:
Synchronous replay,  >=1: background
replay with specified lag, -1:
infinite lag

options.name: --main-memory-replication
options.description: (Default: False) Use main-memory replication model.

options.name: --on-demand-checkpoint
options.description:
(Default: False) Used with main-memory replication
model. Take on demand checkpoint to
avoid missing data when attaching

options.name: --aof-null-device
options.description:
(Default: False) With main-memory replication, use
null device for AOF. Ensures no disk
IO, but can cause data loss during
replication.

options.name: --config-import-path
options.description:
Import (load) configuration options
from the provided path

options.name: --config-import-format
options.description:
(Default: GarnetConf) Format of configuration options in
path specified by config-import-path

options.name: --config-export-format
options.description:
(Default: GarnetConf) Format to export configuration
options to path specified by
config-export-path

options.name: --use-azure-storage-for-config-import
options.description:
(Default: false) Use Azure storage to
import config file

options.name: --config-export-path
options.description:
Export (save) current configuration
options to the provided path

options.name: --use-azure-storage-for-config-export
options.description:
(Default: false) Use Azure storage to
export config file

options.name: --use-native-device-linux
options.description:
(Default: False) Use native device on Linux for local
storage

options.name: --reviv-bin-record-sizes
options.description:
#,#,...,#: For the main store, the
sizes of records in each
revivification bin, in order of
increasing size.           Supersedes
the default --reviv; cannot be used
with --reviv-in-chain-only

options.name: --reviv-bin-record-counts
options.description:
#,#,...,#: For the main store, the
number of records in each bin:
Default (not specified): If
reviv-bin-record-sizes is specified,
each bin is 256 records    # (one
value): If reviv-bin-record-sizes is
specified, then all bins have this
number of records, else error
#,#,...,# (multiple values): If
then it must be the same size as that
array, else error
Supersedes the default --reviv;
cannot be used with

options.name: --reviv-in-chain-only
options.description: Revivify tombstoned records in tag chains only (do not use free list).    Cannot be used with reviv-bin-record-sizes or reviv-bin-record-counts. Propagates to object store by default.

options.name: --reviv-fraction
options.description:
(Default: 1) #: Fraction of mutable in-memory log
space, from the highest log address
down to the read-only region, that is
eligible for revivification.
Applies to both main and object
store.

options.name: --reviv
options.description:
(Default: False) A shortcut to specify revivification
with default power-of-2-sized bins.
This default can be overridden by
--reviv-in-chain-only (Default: False) or by the
combination of reviv-bin-record-sizes
and reviv-bin-record-counts.

options.name: --reviv-search-next-higher-bins
options.description:
(Default: 0) Search this number of next-higher
bins if the search cannot be
satisfied in the best-fitting bin.
Requires --reviv or the combination
of rconeviv-bin-record-sizes and
reviv-bin-record-counts

options.name: --reviv-bin-best-fit-scan-limit
options.description:
(Default: 0) Number of records to scan for best
fit after finding first fit.
of reviv-bin-record-sizes and
reviv-bin-record-counts    0: Use
first fit    #: Limit scan to this
many records after first fit, up to
the record count of the bin

options.name: --reviv-obj-bin-record-count
options.description:
(Default: 256) Number of records in the single free
record bin for the object store. The
Object store has only a single bin,
unlike the main store.        Ignored
unless the main store is using the
free record list.

options.name: --object-scan-count-limit
options.description:
(Default: 1000) Limit of items to return in one
iteration of *SCAN command

options.name: --extension-bin-paths
options.description:
List of directories on server from
which custom command binaries can be
loaded by admin users

options.name: --loadmodulecs
options.description: List of modules to be loaded

options.name: --extension-allow-unsigned
options.description:
(Default: False) Allow loading custom commands from
digitally unsigned assemblies (not
recommended)

options.name: --index-resize-freq
options.description:
(Default: 60) Index resize check frequency in
seconds

options.name: --index-resize-threshold
options.description:
(Default: 50) Overflow bucket count over total
index size in percentage to trigger
index resize

options.name: --fail-on-recovery-error
options.description:
(Default: False) (Default: false) Server bootup should
fail if errors happen during bootup
of AOF and checkpointing

options.name: --lua-memory-management-mode
options.description:
(Default: Native) (Default: Native) Memory management
mode for Lua scripts, must be set to
LimittedNative or Managed to impose
script limits

options.name: --lua-script-memory-limit
options.description:
Memory limit for a Lua instances
while running a script,
lua-memory-management-mode must be
set to something other than Native to
use this flag

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.

options.name: --port
options.description: (Default: 6379) Port to run server on

options.name: --bind
options.description:
IP address to bind server to
(default: any)

options.name: --memory
options.description:
(Default: "16g") Total log memory used in bytes
(rounds down to power of 2)

options.name: --page
options.description: (Default: "32m") Size of each page in bytes (rounds

options.name: --segment
options.description:
(Default: "1g") Size of each log segment in bytes on
disk (rounds down to power of 2)

options.name: --index
options.description: (Default: "128m") Start size of hash index in bytes

options.name: --index-max-size
options.description: Max size of hash index in bytes

options.name: --mutable-percent
options.description:
(Default: 90) Percentage of log memory that is kept
mutable

options.name: --readcache
options.description:
(Default: False) Enables read cache for faster access
to on-disk records.

options.name: --readcache-memory
options.description:
(Default: "1g") Total read cache log memory used in
bytes (rounds down to power of 2)

options.name: --readcache-page
options.description: (Default: "32m") Size of each read cache page in bytes

options.name: --obj-heap-memory
options.description:
Object store heap memory size in
bytes (Sum of size taken up by all
object instances in the heap)

options.name: --obj-log-memory
options.description:
(Default: "32m") Object store log memory used in bytes
(Size of only the log with references
to heap objects, excludes size of
heap memory consumed by the objects
themselves referred to from the log)

options.name: --obj-page
options.description: (Default: "1m") Size of each object store page in

options.name: --obj-segment
options.description:
(Default: "32m") Size of each object store log segment
in bytes on disk (rounds down to
power of 2)

options.name: --obj-index
options.description:
(Default: "16m") Start size of object store hash index
in bytes (rounds down to power of 2)

options.name: --obj-index-max-size
options.description: Max size of object store hash index

options.name: --obj-mutable-percent
options.description:
(Default: 90) Percentage of object store log memory
that is kept mutable

options.name: --obj-readcache
options.description:
(Default: False) Enables object store read cache for
faster access to on-disk records.

options.name: --obj-readcache-log-memory
options.description:
(Default: "32m") Total object store read cache log
memory used in bytes (rounds down to

options.name: --obj-readcache-page
options.description:
(Default: "1m") Size of each object store read cache
page in bytes (rounds down to power
of 2)

options.name: --obj-readcache-heap-memory
options.description:
Object store read cache heap memory
size in bytes (Sum of size taken up
by all object instances in the heap)

options.name: --storage-tier
options.description:
(Default: False) Enable tiering of records (hybrid
log) to storage, to support a
larger-than-memory store. Use
--logdir to specify storage
directory.

options.name: --copy-reads-to-tail
options.description:
(Default: False) When records are read from the main
store's in-memory immutable region or
storage device, copy them to the tail
of the log.

options.name: --obj-copy-reads-to-tail
options.description: (Default: False) When records are read from the object

options.name: --logdir
options.description:
Storage directory for tiered records
(hybrid log), if storage tiering
(--storage) is enabled. Uses current
directory if unspecified.

options.name: --checkpointdir
options.description:
Storage directory for checkpoints.
Uses logdir if unspecified.

options.name: --recover
options.description:
(Default: False) Recover from latest checkpoint and
log, if present.

options.name: --no-pubsub
options.description: (Default: False) Disable pub/sub feature on server.

options.name: --incsnap
options.description: (Default: False) Enable incremental snapshots.

options.name: --pubsub-pagesize
options.description: (Default: "4k") Page size of log used for pub/sub

options.name: --no-obj
options.description:
(Default: False) Disable support for data structure
objects.

options.name: --cluster
options.description: (Default: False) Enable cluster.

options.name: --clean-cluster-config
options.description: (Default: False) Start with clean cluster config.

options.name: --auth
options.description:
(Default: NoAuth) (Default: ACL) Authentication mode of
Garnet. This impacts how AUTH command
is processed and how clients are
authenticated against Garnet. Value
options: NoAuth, Password, Aad, ACL
options: None - no compaction, Shift
